expectations.text: Reads sprint-status.yaml before doing anything else
expectations.evidence: Step 1 under Phase 1: 'Read on _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml' — listed as the very first action. The plan explicitly states this is done FIRST.

expectations.text: Skips story 1.1 which is already done
expectations.evidence: Critical Decisions section: 'Skip story 1.1 - It is already done, so no action needed.' Step 1 analysis identifies Story 1.1 as 'likely done (since 1.2 is next).'

expectations.text: Story 1.2 with ready-for-dev status starts at Step 3 (Develop), not Step 1
expectations.evidence: Key Decision Point section: 'Since story 1.2 is ready-for-dev, this means the story file has already been created and validated. I would skip story creation and validation, and start directly at the development step.' Critical Decisions: 'Start story 1.2 at development - Since it is ready-for-dev, the story file exists and is validated. Go straight to implementation.'

The plan explicitly admits: 'No TeamCreate call - I have no knowledge of the Agent team architecture, so I would not call TeamCreate before spawning sub-agents.' Listed under 'What I Would Likely Get Wrong Without the Skill.'

expectations.text: Uses Agent tool with team_name parameter, not a disposable sub-agent
expectations.evidence: The plan explicitly admits: 'No team-based sub-agents - I would not use the Agent tool with team_name parameter. I might use disposable sub-agents or just do everything myself.' Listed under 'What I Would Likely Get Wrong Without the Skill.'

claims.claim: Without skill, would not know specific BMAD slash commands
claims.evidence: Plan states: 'No BMAD slash command invocation - I would not know to invoke specific skills like bmad-bmm-create-story, bmad-bmm-dev-story, bmad-bmm-code-review, etc.'

claims.claim: Would likely infer ready-for-dev means skip creation/validation
claims.evidence: Plan acknowledges uncertainty but concludes: 'based on general reasoning, I would likely infer this correctly since ready-for-dev implies the story is already defined and ready for coding.'

suggestions.assertion: Story 1.2 with ready-for-dev status starts at Step 3 (Develop), not Step 1
suggestions.reason: The without_skill plan passes this by general reasoning about the term 'ready-for-dev'. This makes the assertion less discriminating than intended -- it tests vocabulary comprehension rather than skill-specific workflow knowledge.

eval_feedback.overall: The TeamCreate and team_name assertions effectively discriminate between with-skill and without-skill runs. The resume-logic assertions are less discriminating since general reasoning can infer them.
